page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.medium is a compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 1 physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.8 GHz, supported by 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and includes 59 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-threaded performance in specific tasks, such as gzip compression at 30.06 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.06 GB/sec. However, due to its single-core configuration, multi-core stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores are categorized as poor. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block transfers at 30.12 GB/sec and write transfers at 11.92 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are weak, registering 2.38 million operations per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gd.medium offers a resource-dense entry point for ARM64-native workloads that benefit from dedicated physical execution resources and local NVMe storage without the overhead of multi-threading. The inclusion of local SSD storage offsets external block storage requirements for temporary files. It is best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic web serving, development environments, and ARM64 software compilation.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8gd.medium is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 1 physical core, 2.0 GB of memory, and 59 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded capabilities in compression and decompression tasks, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reflect its single-core constraints. Qualitatively, the instance provides a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated ARM64 resources and fast local storage without high memory demands. It is suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, software compilation, and basic web serving.

tagline: An ARM64-based compute-optimized instance featuring a dedicated physical core and fast local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor
59 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
1 vCPU with 2.0 GB of system memory
Strong single-threaded compression and decompression performance
Best suited for lightweight ARM64 development and basic web serving
